nodes.name: Cron: 1st @ 09:00 PT
nodes.notes: Runs at 09:00 America/Los_Angeles on the 1st of every month. n8n instance TZ must be set to PT or adjust cron accordingly (17:00 UTC during PDT, 16:00 during PST).

nodes.name: pacer partners payout run
nodes.notes: Runs the CLI. Writes reports/payouts/<period>/ledger_<period>.csv and 1099nec_<year>.csv. Exit code non-zero aborts the workflow.

nodes.name: Read 1099-NEC CSV
nodes.notes: May not exist if no partners crossed the $600 YTD threshold. Continue-on-fail handles that case.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "text": "*PACER monthly payouts — {{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.period }}*
• Entries: *{{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.entry_count }}*
• Partner total: *${{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.partner_total_usd }}*
• Persisted: *{{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.persisted ? 'yes' : 'no (dry-run)' }}*
• Ledger: `{{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.ledger_csv_path }}`
• 1099-NEC: `{{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.nec_csv_path }}`",
  "username": "PACER ops",
  "icon_emoji": ":moneybag:"
}

parameters.subject: =PACER 1099-NEC roster — FY{{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.year }} (period {{ $('Parse CLI Summary').first().json.period }})

nodes.name: Email 1099-NEC roster
nodes.notes: SMTP credentials configured in n8n. Recipients = internal finance + principal engineer. Actual 1099-NEC filing still happens manually via IRS FIRE or Track1099 — this email is the trigger.

meta.description: Monthly partner payout orchestration. Runs the pacer CLI, reads the generated ledger + 1099-NEC CSVs, posts a Slack summary to #pacer-ops, and emails the 1099 roster to finance. Gated on aftermarket_listings_enabled-independent path: the ledger/1099 pipeline runs regardless of listing toggles. Owner: 1COMMERCE LLC (Canby, OR). Compliance: 1099-NEC email is an internal trigger, not the IRS filing itself — actual FIRE/Track1099 submission remains manual.
